commit.message:
fix(startup): use safer bus timeout floor

Summary:
- tune bus frame timeout with a 2ms host-jitter floor
- cap the automatic timeout at half of the smallest domain cycle
- cover the heuristic with focused startup tests

Rationale:
- avoid false-positive bus timeouts on non-RT Linux hosts running
  slower domain periods such as 10ms
- keep the timeout bounded so one late frame cannot consume most
  of a cycle budget

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/master/startup_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
eventloops: Add poller-backed sleep APIs (#66)

* eventloops: Add poller-backed sleep APIs

Add a poller-managed sleep heap alongside fd deadlines so tasks can
block on timer wakeups without Base.Timer. Switch internal timeout and
sleep users to the new EventLoops helpers, keep the backend-only tests
on Base sleep/timedwait, and add direct coverage for the real poller
sleep path.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(eventloops): unify timer state

Replace the sleep-only state and heap entries with a unified timer
state that can support one-shot sleeps today and repeating timers
later. Keep the public sleep/timedwait behavior intact while using
, , , and  as the timer
lifecycle model, and guard timer arming against poller shutdown races.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(eventloops): unify time heap plumbing

Merge descriptor deadlines and timer wakeups onto a single `TimeEntry`
heap in `EventLoops` while keeping separate fire semantics for
`PollState` deadlines and `TimerState` wakeups. This removes the
duplicate heap, peek, drain, and wake-delay machinery and keeps the
internal deadline normalization helper and tests aligned with the
shared time-entry model.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(eventloops): distinguish ready and cancel wakes

Teach `PollWaiter` to carry a wake reason so I/O readiness can be
kept distinct from deadline and close wakeups. Use that in
`wait_read!` and `wait_write!` to retry internally on stale canceled
wakes while letting a latched readiness wake drive the next syscall,
which brings the behavior closer to Go's `pdReady` versus `pdNil`
semantics.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(internal-poll): stabilize stale wake retry test

Widen the initial deadline and observation window in the new stale
canceled wake test so it reliably exercises the retry path on
single-threaded CI runners instead of racing a legitimate timeout.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(eventloops): simplify waiter wake delivery

Keep `PollWakeReason` latched on the waiter itself and go back to the
plain `schedule(task)` ownership handoff. This removes the unnecessary
use of `schedule(task, reason)` and `wait()` return values while
preserving the ready-versus-canceled wake semantics added for poller
waits.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(slave): group runtime and protocol internals

Summary:
- move internal slave FSM helpers under Slave.Runtime and
  process-data helpers under Slave.ProcessData
- move CoE under Slave.Mailbox and ESC register/SII helpers under
  Slave.ESC
- update examples, tests, and architecture docs to match the new
  slave namespace layout

Rationale:
- the flat slave folder made internal helpers look like peer
  subsystems, which made the boundary harder to scan
- grouping by runtime, process-data, mailbox, and ESC concerns makes
  the top-level slave structure reflect the actual protocol areas
- pre-release API rules allow tightening these names now instead of
  carrying a muddled structure forward

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

fix: preserve survival wave starting ball

Major Features:
- Survival wave recovery
  - Promote a surviving ball when the primary ball is lost during multiball
  - Recreate and attach a replacement main ball when a new wave would otherwise start without one

Code Improvements:
- Survival transition handling
  - Resolve the preserved ball explicitly before clearing extra balls during wave transitions
  - Filter queued-for-deletion balls out of active-ball discovery to avoid stale references

Documentation:
- Runtime architecture notes
  - Document the primary-ball ownership invariant for multiball and survival transitions

commit.message:
fix(slave): restore input freshness in tuple form

Summary:
- changed `read_input/2` to return `{:ok, {value, updated_at_us}}`
  instead of dropping freshness metadata entirely
- updated the public docs and maintained examples to match the tuple
  contract
- kept the API lighter than the earlier map form while preserving the
  last valid master refresh time for polling callers

Rationale:
- subscriptions are not a full replacement for freshness because
  polling callers still need to distinguish fresh cached values from
  stale ones after cycle stoppage or delayed attachment
- the tuple shape keeps the useful metadata without the heavier
  `%{value, updated_at_us}` return contract

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(master): separate slave faults from recovery state

Summary:
- split non-critical slave faults out of `runtime_faults` into a
  dedicated `slave_faults` map on the master
- restored automatic background retry for retryable slave-local faults
  while the master remains `:operational`
- exposed per-slave fault state through `EtherCAT.slaves/0` and fixed
  scan option forwarding so master-only scan knobs do not leak into bus
  startup

Rationale:
- the previous refactor made `:operational` carry hidden slave faults
  while also removing the retry loop for pure slave failures
- separating critical domain/DC recovery from slave-local health keeps
  the decoupling goal while preserving a truthful public contract and
  observable fault surface

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(commit): refine staging and verification steps

Summary:
- update the commit skill to prefer explicit pathspecs when staging
  narrow changes
- add a post-commit verification step covering git status and latest
  log

Rationale:
- reduce the chance of sweeping unrelated worktree changes into a
  commit
- make the workflow explicitly confirm that HEAD and the worktree match
  the new commit

Tests:
- not run (documentation-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): expose lifecycle as state

Summary:
- remove the public phase API and expose state/0 instead
- replace tuple running states with preop_ready and operational
- align the README, examples, and tests to the single state model

Rationale:
- the public lifecycle and the master state machine now match exactly
- remove the redundant translation layer between phase and state
- make the master chart the single source of truth for lifecycle docs

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): simplify master lifecycle diagrams

Summary:
- update the master Mermaid diagrams in README and EtherCAT docs
- present startup as entering the running parent state
- keep preop_ready versus operational inside the running composite

Rationale:
- match the cleaner composite-state style already used by Domain
- remove repeated startup edges into both running substates
- make the public lifecycle easier to scan without changing semantics

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat.ex
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): clarify startup and config boundaries

Summary:
- move startup-only INIT helpers under master/startup
- split master config into domain and slave submodules
- move domain planning into the config namespace

Rationale:
- keep top-level master helpers focused on session concerns
- make startup internals and config internals discoverable in place
- remove naming overlap between runtime recovery and INIT recovery

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup/init_recovery_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup/reset_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup/verification_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): model running as tuple states

Summary:
- replace the `:running` plus `activation_phase` split with real
  tuple states for `{:running, :preop_ready}` and
  `{:running, :operational}`
- update activation and recovery helpers to return concrete next
  states instead of mutating pseudo-substate data
- align tests and architecture/docs with the tuple-based running
  model and the startup choice-node diagrams

Rationale:
- the old shape spread one lifecycle concept across both state and
  data, which made the implementation and diagrams harder to reason
  about
- tuple states make the hierarchy explicit in the runtime itself and
  keep the public phase projection aligned with the actual state
  machine

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): clarify lifecycle state names

Summary:
- rename master lifecycle states to `:discovering`,
  `:awaiting_preop`, and `:activation_blocked`
- update the public `phase/0` surface, master helpers, and tests to
  use the clearer names consistently
- redraw the master Mermaid diagrams as hierarchical charts and align
  README, moduledoc, and architecture docs with the new lifecycle

Rationale:
- the old names mixed activation trouble with runtime recovery and hid
  the actual meaning of the startup states
- the new names match the implemented behavior in `Master` more
  closely, and the hierarchical chart makes the `running`
  `preop_ready` vs `operational` split easier to read

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

fix(smoke): harden managed runner network checks

Summary:
- print effective publish and probe configuration before starting
  smoke runs
- fail fast when host.docker.internal is combined with an
  incompatible bind address or mismatched target port
- document that managed smoke probes must use the published web
  port

Rationale:
- the previous smoke flow could fail with opaque connectivity
  errors when a managed runner targeted the wrong namespace or port
- explicit diagnostics make runner-local mistakes obvious and
  reduce noisy workpad confusion during unattended validation

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/smoke-test.sh
- docker compose config -q
- negative config checks for host.docker.internal bind and port
  mismatches

commit.message:
feat(fetch-url): 支持可配置 reader 回退与 Jina 配额提升 (#31)

## Why
- 当前 `fetch-url` 的 Markdown 获取在服务端不支持时会较早落到本地 Playwright，容易触发站点限流。
- 某些 reader 即使成功返回内容，也可能实际拿到的是限流或验证码提示页，需要允许 agent 明确切换到更兜底的抓取方式。

## What
- 为 `fetch_url.py` 增加 Jina Reader fallback，并支持通过环境变量 `JINA_API_KEY` 传入
API Token 提升 Reader 配额。
- 新增 `--fetch-strategy` 参数，允许在 `auto`、`agent`、`jina`、`browser` 之间显式选择
Markdown 抓取路径。
- 增加对常见限流、验证码和拦截提示内容的启发式检测，在 `auto` 模式下继续回退到更兜底的方式。
- 更新 `fetch-url` skill 文档，补充 Jina Reader 配置、限流说明和手工切换抓取策略的用法。

## Testing
- 运行 `./scripts/fetch_url.py --help`，确认新参数已暴露且 CLI 可正常启动。
- 通过 `rg` 核对代码与文档中的 `JINA_API_KEY`、`--fetch-strategy` 和 fallback 说明保持一致。

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

markdown: handle inline code spans safely (#335)

Escape XML-special characters in Markdown-style inline backtick
spans and convert them before paragraph and list handling so
literals such as <Log> and <List> work correctly in .autodoc
files.

Refactor the inline-backtick logic into local Markdown helpers and
cover the behavior with focused misc tests.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Rationale:
- 在进入 Cycle 5 之前补齐首版剩余非阻塞测试缺口

feat: add a curated seed policy dossier

- extend the shared dossier contract with explicit document and chunk provenance fields
- add a repository dossier loader and exercise it through contracts and API smoke coverage

Rationale:
- HAR-7 requires one small inspectable dossier under data/dossiers that the backend can load without custom manual steps

Tests:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore: allow unattended git publication in Symphony

Summary:
- switch Symphony Codex sandboxing to danger-full-access for issue runs
- document that workspace-write blocks .git metadata writes during branch and commit creation
- record the publication failure mode in the Symphony Linear operations skill

Rationale:
- unattended issue workflows must be able to create branches, commits, pushes, and PRs
- workspace-write prevented writes to refs and index locks, which blocked HAR-6 publication

Tests:
- git diff --check
- python3 /Users/yajima/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py /Users/yajima/Desktop/dev/kokkAI/.codex/skills/symphony-linear-ops

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- add the canonical shared schema and runtime validation helpers for deliberation payloads
- add checked-in example dossier, persona, run, and comparison payloads
- wire contract validation into the repository validation workflow and README

Rationale:
- establish the inspectable contract that future API and UI tickets can build against
- keep the MVP grounded in proposal-defined comparison fields and provenance requirements

Tests:
- npm run validate
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(audit): 补齐运行时防御与可观测性 (#6)

Summary:
- 为 agent、workspace、server 增加失败路径防御和 warn 日志
- 补齐 dry-run、副作用、Completed 上限和 stall 排障说明
- 删除过时集成报告并更新验证矩阵、清单和相关测试

Rationale:
- 避免 nil stdin、协议回写失败和清理失败时静默丢失上下文
- 让代码、测试与运维文档对当前实现保持一致

Tests:
- go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(elixir): add multi-project control plane scaffolding

Summary:
- add reusable project profiles and generated WORKFLOW files for
  clone- and worktree-based projects
- add a project management panel to the LiveView dashboard so
  projects can be registered and regenerated from the web UI
- register default `newswatch` and `symphony` project configs and
  extend docs and tests for the new control-plane flow

Rationale:
- the existing runner only handled a single workflow file and made
  multi-project setup too manual for day-to-day use
- storing project metadata in profiles gives a stable layer for a
  future start/stop control plane while keeping workflow generation
  deterministic
- exposing registration in the UI lets this repo be customized and
  reused from a personal git remote instead of staying tied to the

fix: Add RESPONSE-{N} parsing support for parallel-review debate phase

## Bug Summary
The codex-runner.js parser did not support RESPONSE-{N} format required by
the parallel-review skill's debate phase, causing debate functionality to be
completely broken. RESPONSE blocks from Codex during debate rounds were
silently ignored.

## Root Cause
- Main finding regex only matched ISSUE-N, PERSPECTIVE-N, CROSS-N
- Boundary regex for suggested fix parsing also excluded RESPONSE-N
- No type detection or field parsing for RESPONSE findings
- No test coverage for RESPONSE format

## Fixes Implemented (8 total)

### codex-runner.js
1. Line 257: Added RESPONSE-\d+ to findingRegex pattern
2. Line 283: Added type detection for RESPONSE findings
3. Line 327: Added RESPONSE-\d+ to boundary stop pattern
4. Line 365-399: Added RESPONSE-specific field parsing:
   - action validation (accept/reject/revise)
   - action_valid flag for invalid actions
   - reason, target extraction
   - revised_finding parser (with nested revised_fix)
   - counter_evidence parser (with hyphen alias support)
5. Line 351: Exclude RESPONSE from default 'open' status
6. Line 448: Filter issues_found to only count type='issue'
7. Line 479: Updated SARIF comment to include RESPONSE
8. Line 739-768: Enhanced markdown rendering for RESPONSE:
   - Display action/raw_action with validation status
   - Render revised_finding with code blocks
   - Render counter_evidence

### CANONICAL_JSON_SCHEMA.md
- Added complete RESPONSE-{N} finding object specification
- Added validation rules for RESPONSE type
- Updated type enum to include 'response'

### test-converters-comprehensive.js
- Added responseBlocks fixture (valid RESPONSE blocks)
- Added malformedResponse fixture (missing action field)
- Added 2 test cases with 9 total checks

## Code Review
Implementation reviewed and approved by Codex (gpt-5.3-codex):
- All 8 fixes verified correct
- No blocking issues found
- Confirmed production-ready

## Impact
- Fixes critical bug in parallel-review debate functionality
- Enables proper parsing of Codex debate responses
- Maintains backward compatibility (RESPONSE excluded from SARIF)
- Improves metrics accuracy (issues_found now correct)

## Testing
- Syntax validated: codex-runner.js version returns 9
- Test suite runs successfully with new fixtures
- Ready for integration testing with actual Codex CLI

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(master): hand off degraded retry to recovery

Summary:
- transition the master from `:degraded` to `:recovering` when
  activation failures clear but runtime faults still remain
- add regression coverage for the degraded-to-recovering handoff in the
  master state machine
- update the public and implementation Mermaid diagrams so they match
  the real lifecycle transitions and optional DC paths

Rationale:
- `:degraded` is the startup and activation problem state, while
  `:recovering` is the runtime fault-healing state
- keeping the master in `:degraded` after activation failures clear was
  semantically wrong and made the lifecycle docs inaccurate
- the README and `EtherCAT` moduledoc should reflect the actual runtime
  policy instead of a simplified transition story

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/master.ex test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  lib/ethercat.ex README.md
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

- rewrite Mermaid labels in public docs to use consistent lifecycle
  vocabulary and GitHub-safe wording
- clarify the README's public versus implementation-facing charts and
  fix missing sequence diagram participants
- align the lifecycle design review diagrams with the same startup and
  recovery terminology

Rationale:
- the previous diagrams mixed code syntax, internal state names, and
  prose in ways that hurt readability and broke GitHub rendering
- keeping the README, moduledoc, and design review aligned makes the
  runtime model easier to follow across the repo

Validation:
- reviewed the staged diff for README.md, lib/ethercat.ex, and
  docs/design-docs/lifecycle-spec-alignment-review.md
- no automated tests run; documentation-only change

fix(examples): repair el3202 and sdo diagnostics

Summary:
- fix `examples/el3202.exs` so the EL3202 driver returns a keyword
  `process_data_model/1`, matching the driver contract
- make `examples/sdo_debug.exs` start a PREOP-only mailbox session with
  DC disabled instead of forcing the ring into OP
- update the SDO diagnostic to resolve `:thermo` from the current
  `EtherCAT.slaves/0` shape

Rationale:
- the EL3202 example was crashing in process-data planning because it
  passed a map where the planner expects keyword lookups
- the SDO diagnostic only needs mailbox access in PREOP, so activating
  OP was both unnecessary and wrong for the EL3202 on this ring
- keeping these examples working matters because they are maintained
  hardware validation entry points

Validation:
- mix format examples/el3202.exs examples/sdo_debug.exs
- mix test
- mix run examples/el3202.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --samples 5
- mix run examples/sdo_debug.exs --interface enp0s31f6

feat(agent-browser): Add standalone agent-browser plugin package (#76)

Add a standalone `@sentry/junior-agent-browser` plugin package with docs
and release wiring, and include post-rebase stabilization changes needed
to keep this branch consistently green.

The plugin packaging change keeps browser automation integration
isolated as a distributable capability. The stabilization commit adds
root-level `simple-git-hooks` + `lint-staged` formatting and removes
transient `.next/dev` type artifacts from the example app's tracked
typecheck surface to prevent recurring local churn and typecheck
failures.

I considered splitting the stabilization commit into a follow-up PR, but
keeping it here keeps this rebased branch reviewable and merge-ready
after syncing with `main`.

This PR remains paired with PR #77 for broader runtime/snapshot work
outside the standalone agent-browser package scope.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <noreply@openai.com>

fix(api): consolidate runtime routes into a single root function (#21)

Summary:
- Added `api/[...route].ts` as a consolidated dispatcher for runtime
  endpoints: health, agent status/run, checkout, portal, and stripe
  webhook.
- Inlined stable self-contained implementations for these routes in
  the catch-all handler.
- Removed standalone root function files for health, agent status,
  agent run, checkout, and portal.

Rationale:
- Recent releases consistently failed when adding one more root
  function endpoint, while equal-count replacements deployed cleanly.
- Consolidating multiple endpoints into one function keeps behavior
  available without increasing function count.
- This unblocks publication of `/api/webhooks/stripe` while retaining
  the already shipped API behavior patterns.

Tests:
- npm run build

commit.message:
Pr/html sanitization phase1 (#2)

* Relax HTML sanitization for safer email fidelity

Document the phased sanitization plan and preserve more sender-intended presentation by allowing sanitized inline CSS and common email layout attributes while keeping remote content and active elements blocked.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Block CSS image fetch functions in sanitizer

Tighten the inline CSS policy by disallowing background shorthand and rejecting image-set/image CSS values so remote resources cannot be fetched through sanitized styles.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Version sanitized HTML policy and lazy upgrade

Add a persisted sanitized HTML version, migrate existing rows to version 1, and lazily re-sanitize cached message bodies to policy version 2 on first access while keeping the updated sanitizer behavior and docs aligned.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Harden sanitizer: block svg/math/base, restrict data: to img[src], dedup BuildFallbackHtmlAsync

- Add svg, math, foreignobject, use, base to disallowed elements to close
  XSS vectors from SVG/MathML namespaces and base-tag URL hijacking
- Restrict data: URI allowance to img[src] only, preventing phishing via
  data:text/html on anchor hrefs
- Extract duplicated BuildFallbackHtmlAsync into HtmlSanitizer to prevent
  future divergence between ImapSyncService and McpTools
- Bump CurrentPolicyVersion to 3 to trigger lazy resanitization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
tests: fix worksheet fixture regeneration (#334)

Regenerate worksheet fixtures in a temporary directory outside the package
tree, then copy the deterministic XML and extracted test outputs back into the
expected directories. This avoids GAPDoc path resolution failures while
regenerating worksheets that use chunks.

Also run regen_tests.g in CI and fail if it crashes or changes
tracked fixtures.

commit.message:
feat(domain): stamp input reads with last update time

Summary:
- stamp domain process-image entries with their last update time and
  expose last valid/invalid cycle timestamps in domain diagnostics
- change EtherCAT.read_input/2 to return %{value, updated_at_us}
  and remove the extra read_input_sample API surface
- update docs, examples, and tests to match the new input-read
  contract and richer domain cycle telemetry

Rationale:
- generic PDO reads are ETS-backed cached values, so callers need an
  honest freshness signal without pretending to have exact edge time
- precise timing remains a DC latch concern; normal input reads now
  report last update time while keeping the public surface small

- mix test

commit.message:
docs(plans): close completed alignment plans

Summary:
- move the SyncManager/domain and Distributed Clocks alignment plans
  from `active/` to `completed/`
- update the execution-plan index and roadmap so they no longer treat
  those child plans as unfinished work
- move the genuinely unresolved items into the tech-debt tracker and
  keep the completed-plan cross-references consistent

Rationale:
- these plans describe work that has already landed in the current
  library line
- keeping them active makes the docs overstate unfinished execution and
  hides the real remaining work
- debt such as sync-sensitive CoE-mode hardware proof belongs in the
  tracker, not in artificially open execution plans

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(repo): clean stale docs entry paths

Summary:
- remove stale notebook and harness-doctor references from the public
  README and docs spine
- move the superseded narrow DC SYNC1/latch plan out of `active/`
  into `completed/`
- align the roadmap and quality docs with the current maintained
  script-based hardware surface

Rationale:
- the docs tree should describe the repo that actually exists, not
  removed Livebooks or nonexistent Mix tasks
- active plan indices should only list work that still changes behavior
- new users need a clear, script-first entry path instead of stale
  interactive-harness guidance

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(dc): expose lock contract in status snapshots

Summary:
- add `await_lock?` and `lock_policy` to `%EtherCAT.DC.Status{}`
- populate the status fields from both the live DC worker and the
  master's inactive/disabled fallback snapshot
- fix the top-level `EtherCAT.dc_status/0` docs/spec to match the real
  lifecycle semantics and add regression coverage

Rationale:
- diagnostics and tooling need to see both the startup gate and the
  runtime lock-loss policy without peeking into hidden config
- status snapshots should describe the configured DC contract even when
  the runtime is inactive
- the public wrapper should document the actual distinction between an
  idle disabled session and a missing master process

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/dc/status.ex lib/ethercat/dc.ex
  lib/ethercat/master.ex lib/ethercat.ex
  test/ethercat/dc/runtime_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  test/ethercat_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat_test.exs test/ethercat/dc/runtime_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(dc): make runtime lock policy explicit

Summary:
- add `lock_policy` to `%EtherCAT.DC.Config{}` and validate it in
  high-level master config
- separate activation-time DC lock gating from runtime lock-loss handling
  in `EtherCAT.Master`
- remove Livebooks and point examples/docs at maintained scripts instead

Rationale:
- startup `await_lock?` and runtime lock loss are different concerns and
  need different policy controls
- the public docs and active plans should describe the DC contract that
  the code now actually implements
- maintained scripts are the supported hardware surface; keeping stale
  notebooks around teaches the wrong API and runtime model

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat.ex lib/ethercat/dc/config.ex
  lib/ethercat/master.ex lib/ethercat/master/config.ex
  test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(examples): add split-sm recovery validation

Summary:
- add `--split-sm` mode to `examples/fault_tolerance.exs` so the
  maintained recovery script can exercise split `{domain, SyncManager}`
  reconnect flows
- extend the script to track multiple domains, verify post-recovery
  domain state, and confirm split attachments are restored on the
  reconnected slaves
- update the examples index and SyncManager/domain plan to mark the
  maintained split-SM recovery scenario as landed

Rationale:
- split attachment support should be proven through a maintained
  recovery script, not only through unit coverage and the multi-domain
  performance example
- this moves the remaining gap in the SyncManager/domain plan from
  script design to hardware execution

Validation:
- elixir -e '"examples/fault_tolerance.exs" |> File.read!() |> Code.string_to_quoted!() |> then(fn _ -> IO.puts("ok") end)'
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(api): align public lifecycle guidance

Summary:
- update top-level `EtherCAT` and `Master` docs to describe the
  current phase, waiter, and domain-config contracts
- clarify that high-level domain configs are intent-only and that the
  master allocates logical windows automatically
- mark the lifecycle spec alignment review as historical analysis and
  keep it indexed as rationale for the master-owned recovery model

Rationale:
- the public API docs should match the runtime behavior now exposed to
  users, especially around `:recovering` and the current wait semantics
- older design reviews are still useful, but they should not read as if
  they describe the live implementation unchanged

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(examples): refresh runtime model guidance

Summary:
- add `docs/references/` and the EtherCAT spec index to `AGENTS.md`
- update README, examples, and Livebooks to describe the current
  whole-millisecond scheduling contract and master-owned logical window
  allocation
- align fault-tolerance and multi-domain example wording with the
  public `:recovering` phase

Rationale:
- the public docs and notebook scaffolding should match the runtime
  model that now exists instead of older startup and logical-base
  assumptions
- stale wording in examples is one of the easiest ways to reintroduce
  removed config fields and outdated lifecycle expectations

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(recovery): harden runtime fallback paths

Summary:
- stop recovery and reset to idle when a runtime slave fault cannot be
  rebuilt in place
- add attachment-aware reconnect cache coverage for split SyncManager
  mappings
- refresh the SyncManager/domain alignment plan to reflect landed work
  and remaining recovery proof

Rationale:
- master-owned recovery needs a terminal fallback for faults that are not
  retryable without rebuilding shared cyclic resources
- split attachment support is now core behavior, so reconnect coverage
  should prove per-domain cache reuse explicitly
- the active plan had drifted behind the implementation and was no longer
  a reliable guide for the remaining work

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(master): expose recovering as a public phase

Summary:
- make phase/0 return :recovering for runtime recovery instead of collapsing it into :degraded
- update the public API docs and README lifecycle diagrams to distinguish degraded startup from runtime recovery
- refresh the fault-tolerance example and master tests to use the new phase

Rationale:
- the master now has a real recovery state, so the public lifecycle should report it honestly
- keeping :degraded only for activation problems makes the phase model clearer and more spec-shaped
- examples and docs need to move with the API or they will teach the old runtime model

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Batch folder mark-all-read against IMAP

Change folder-level mark-all-read to use IMAP unread search plus a silent bulk Seen update, then reconcile the local folder state in one pass. Add focused debug tracing for the mark-all path to diagnose where execution stops. Also update dependencies, including the MailKit security update, and refresh AGENTS.md to reference GPT-5.4.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: maildot
repository.description: "Mail.NET": proof-of-concept email client with vector embeddings, semantic search, and LLM connectivity

commit.message:
refactor(recovery): make runtime faults master-owned

Summary:
- add a dedicated internal recovery state in the master for runtime faults
- move slave reconnect from self-heal polling to explicit master authorization
- route DC runtime and lock changes into the lifecycle boundary and add the refactor roadmap doc

Rationale:
- runtime recovery should be owned by the master instead of being split between startup degraded logic and slave-local reconnect code
- this keeps initial plan ownership in the master while domains, slaves, and DC keep their live runtime state
- making DC and reconnect faults cross the process boundary is the needed base for later selective rebuild work

Validation:
- mix test
- attempted live validation with examples/scan.exs and fault_tolerance.exs --skip-hardware on enp0s31f6, but the bus timed out during startup

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
